channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Critique of UK Home Secretary's Claim on Permanent Refugee Status in Proposed Asylum Reforms
item.description: The article critiques recent UK asylum reforms announced by Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood, which introduce temporary refugee status subject to review every 30 months. Mahmood has described current ru...

item.title: Trump Orders Military Action Against Iran Citing Pre-emptive Threat
item.description: United States President Donald Trump has triggered a major conflict with Iran, reportedly based on his own instincts rather than formal analysis or strategic planning. According to statements from Whi...

item.title: The Structural Barriers to Reforming Iran’s Government
item.description: Efforts to reform Iran from within have consistently failed due to the foundational structure of its political system, particularly the doctrine of velayat-e faqih. This principle, which places ultima...

item.title: Virginia enacts law requiring schools to teach January 6 as attack on democracy
item.description: Virginia ke legislature ne naya law pass kiya hai jo public schools ko mana karta hai ke wo former President Donald Trump ka nazariya January 6, 2021 US Capitol attack ke bare mein padhain. Is law ke ...

item.title: Kenya’s Monica Juma appointed head of UN Office on Drugs and Crime
item.description: The UN Secretary-General António Guterres has officially named Kenya’s Monica Juma as the new head of the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC) as well as the United Nations Office at Vienn...

item.title: Health Benefits and Limits of Honey for Recovery and Wellbeing
item.description: Honey has long been celebrated for potential health benefits, including soothing sore throats, aiding sleep, healing wounds, and supporting general wellbeing. Chemically, honey is around 20% water and...

item.title: Glyphosate Detected in Staple Foods and Infant Cereals in South Africa, Experts Call for Regulation
item.description: The African Centre for Biodiversity (ACB) has urged Agriculture Minister John Steenhuisen to ban glyphosate and glyphosate-based herbicides (GBHs) following new evidence showing the chemicals are pres...

item.title: Report reveals 146 injuries at Liverpool FC title parade unrelated to Water Street car attack
item.description: A new independent report into the organisation of Liverpool FC's Premier League victory parade on 26 May 2025 has revealed that 146 people required medical treatment during the event, separate from th...

item.title: Iranian warship IRIS Lavan docks in Kochi following US attack on IRIS Dena
item.description: An Iranian warship, IRIS Lavan, has arrived at the port of Kochi in Kerala, a day after the US attacked and sank another Iranian vessel, IRIS Dena, near Sri Lankan waters. According to reports, India ...

item.title: Posta Rangers, Gor Mahia Coaches Exchange Words After 1-1 Draw
item.description: In Nairobi, tensions flared after Posta Rangers and Gor Mahia played to a 1-1 draw in their midweek Kenya Premier League match at Nyayo National Stadium. Posta Rangers head coach Charles Omondi critic...

item.title: Milan Cortina Winter Paralympics Open Amid Controversy Over Russian Flag Return and Boycotts
item.description: The Milan Cortina 2026 Winter Paralympics officially opened on March 6 in Verona, Italy, at the historic Arena di Verona, marking the 50th anniversary of the Winter Paralympic Games. The event feature...

item.title: Robinhood Ventures Fund Raises $658 Million, Faces Challenges in NYSE Launch
item.description: Robinhood has launched its first startup investment fund, Robinhood Ventures Fund I, aiming to give retail investors access to high-profile private companies. The fund includes stakes in Databricks, S...

item.title: CRDB invests Sh560 billion to support over 100,000 women entrepreneurs in Tanzania
item.description: CRDB Bank has successfully disbursed over Sh560 billion to more than 100,000 women entrepreneurs across Tanzania through a special business financing window. The initiative aims to empower women econo...

item.title: Reports Suggest Russia Shares Military Intelligence with Iran on U.S. Targets in the Middle East
item.description: According to reports from the Washington Post, Russia is allegedly sharing intelligence with Iran regarding the location of U.S. military assets in the Middle East, including ships and aircraft. U.S. ...

item.title: Asake Gifts Father 2025 Toyota Land Cruiser Days After Gifting Mother Mercedes G-Wagon
item.description: Afrobeats superstar Asake don show say e dey generous well well as e gift im papa one brand new 2025 Toyota Land Cruiser SUV wey cost pass ₦200 million. Dis one come just few days after e give im mama...

item.title: Saharan dust causes striking reddish skies and 'blood rain' in southern England
item.description: A plume of Saharan dust has drifted into the airspace above the UK, creating unusual and striking skies, particularly across the east of England. The dust, carried north by southerly winds following S...

item.title: Ian Huntley, Soham double murderer, taken off life support and close to death after prison attack
item.description: Ian Huntley, the convicted murderer of schoolgirls Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman in Soham in 2002, is reportedly close to death after being taken off life support. The 52-year-old, who has been serv...

item.title: EJ Obiena wins gold at ISTAF Indoor, clears season-best 5.78 meters
item.description: Filipino pole vaulter EJ Obiena delivered a remarkable performance at the ISTAF Indoor athletics meet, claiming the gold medal with a season-best jump of 5.78 meters. This achievement demonstrates Obi...

item.title: Notable Book Releases for March 2026
item.description: March 2026 brings an exciting mix of new book releases across fiction and non-fiction. Former NSW premier Bob Carr reflects on grief and life after loss in 'Bring Back Yesterday', a deeply personal me...

item.title: Pentagon and Anthropic Disagree on Use of AI in Autonomous Weapons
item.description: A top Pentagon official, Emil Michael, who is the chief technology officer, has revealed a significant disagreement with AI company Anthropic regarding the military use of its AI system, Claude. The d...
